ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for black sesame toast. 'Bread and similar products' (A000E) used as closest parent category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Traditional Black Sesame Toast consists of two slices of toasted white bread spread with black sesame paste and butter, weighing approximately 100 g.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, white, commercially prepared (toasted) and sesame paste spread — composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy) and HPB Singapore Foodo
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Traditional Black Sesame Toast exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for toasted white bread (FDC ID 325871) with USDA data for sesame paste/tahini (FDC ID 168604) in approximate proportions reflecting a typical Singaporean kaya-toast-style preparation with black sesame spread: approximately 70% toasted white bread and 30% black sesame paste by weight. Values are cross-referenced against HPB Singapore Foodo general toast entries where available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and sesame-based analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Traditional Black Sesame Toast. GI of 70 is estimated using white bread as the primary analogous food (GI 70–75, University of Sydney GI Database). The black sesame paste component contributes fat and protein that may modestly lower the effective GI; however, white bread dominates the carbohydrate load (~70% of composition), so GI 70 is retained as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 46.0) / 100 = 32.2 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of Traditional Black Sesame Toast, as its sodium content of approximately 320 mg per 100 g contributes meaningfully toward the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; choosing lower-sodium bread and reducing added butter can help manage intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and significant carbohydrate load (~46 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Traditional Black Sesame Toast in small portions and pair it with a protein or fibre source to blunt the postprandial glucose response, given its high GI of approximately 70 and carbohydrate content of 46 g per 100 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.2 g per 100 g) and sodium (~320 mg per 100 g); partially offset by sesame-derived polyunsaturated fats (~3.5 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of Traditional Black Sesame Toast due to its saturated fat and sodium content, while noting that the black sesame paste contributes beneficial polyunsaturated fatty acids and plant sterols that may partially offset cardiovascular risk when consumed in reasonable portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Traditional Black Sesame Toast is a popular Singaporean breakfast and snack item served at local toast cafes such as Toast Box, Ya Kun Kaya Toast, and similar kopitiam-style establishments. It consists of two slices of thin white bread that are toasted until golden and crisp, then generously spread with a rich black sesame paste made from ground roasted black sesame seeds blended with sugar and sometimes butter or oil. The result is a warm, crunchy toast with a nutty, slightly sweet, and earthy flavour profile characteristic of black sesame. The texture contrasts the crisp exterior of the toasted bread with the smooth, dense sesame paste. Black sesame (Sesamum indicum) is prized in Chinese culinary tradition for its distinctive deep flavour and is associated with various health benefits in traditional medicine. Nutritionally, Traditional Black Sesame Toast provides moderate energy (~320 kcal per 100 g), carbohydrates (~46 g), protein (~7.8 g), and fat (~11.5 g), with notable contributions of iron (~2.8 mg), calcium (~85 mg), and magnesium (~38 mg) from the sesame paste. Its glycemic index is high (~70), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for those with hypertension. It is typically served alongside a soft-boiled egg and a cup of kopi (local coffee) or teh (tea) as part of a traditional Singaporean breakfast set.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Traditional Black Sesame Toast was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from a composite of USDA SR Legacy data for toasted white bread and sesame paste/tahini in proportions typical of a Singaporean black sesame toast preparation. GI is estimated by analogy with white bread from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for toasted white bread (FDC ID 325871) and sesame paste/tahini (FDC ID 168604) in approximately 70:30 proportions. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite food in the source databases. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as toasted white bread and sesame paste contain negligible vitamin C. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white bread (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for Traditional Black Sesame Toast is available in the published literature. Halal status assumed based on typical Toast Box menu declarations; kosher status not confirmed. Serving size set to 100 g for per-100g alignment; typical restaurant serving is approximately 2 slices (~80–110 g).
